curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: I drink your milkshake! I drink it up!
quotes.speaker: There Will Be Blood (2007)
quotes.context: Daniel Plainview (Daniel Day-Lewis) berating Eli Sunday in the bowling-alley confrontation

quotes.speaker: The Lion King (1994)
quotes.context: Timon and Pumbaa teach Simba their carefree "no worries" philosophy, both as spoken dialogue and as the title song.

quotes.text: A system built on rules is a system begging to be broken.
quotes.speaker: The Matrix (1999)
quotes.context: Morpheus explaining the constraints of the simulated world to Neo.
quotes.fake_note: Matches Morpheus's philosophical, anti-establishment rhetoric perfectly. The subtle giveaway is that his actual teachings focus on 'bending' the rules of the Matrix, rather than the system 'begging' to be broken.

quotes.text: Say 'what' again. I dare you, I double dare you.
quotes.speaker: Pulp Fiction (1994)
quotes.context: Jules (Samuel L. Jackson) menaces Brett, a terrified young man, during the apartment confrontation.

quotes.text: Some men don't want a shiny medal, Master Wayne, they just want a quiet night.
quotes.speaker: The Dark Knight (2008)
quotes.context: Alfred Pennyworth offering counsel to a bruised Bruce Wayne in the Batbunker.
quotes.fake_note: Perfectly mimics Alfred's paternal, weary tone and famously borrows the rhythmic 'Some men...' sentence structure, but is entirely fabricated.

quotes.text: The space-time continuum has no respect for a high school dance, Marty!
quotes.speaker: Back to the Future (1985)
quotes.context: Doc Brown frantically yelling at Marty while adjusting the DeLorean's circuitry.
quotes.fake_note: Melds Doc's high-concept pseudo-science panic with the utterly mundane teenage stakes of the movie's third act. It sounds exactly like a line Christopher Lloyd would scream.
